subject: Product Management
title: Fake Door Tests
description: A quick, practical guide to Fake Door Tests.

hook.text: What if you could measure demand for a feature before building it? Fake door tests do exactly that.

buildup.text: Add a button for a feature that doesn't exist yet. When users click it, show a message: 'Coming soon! Interested?'

discovery.text: Click-through rate reveals real demand. If 15% of users click, there's strong interest. If 0.5% click, skip it.

twist.text: Fake doors feel deceptive, but they're far more ethical than building a full feature nobody wants.

climax.text: Amazon, Facebook, and Google all use fake door tests to validate ideas before investing engineering resources.

punchline.text: Measure demand before you build. One button is enough.

quiz.question: What is a fake door test?
options: Adding a button for a non-existent feature to measure real user demand
A usability test with a fake user interface
A test where engineers pretend to build something
